title: moerenhoutia tidorensis
intro: Moerenhoutia tidorensis, also known as the Tidorea or Tidore nut, is a small, deciduous tree native to the tropical forests of Asia. It is a type of evergreen shrub or small tree that belongs to the Rutaceae family. The medicinal properties of Moerenhoutia tidorensis include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions, and it can be prepared as a herbal tea, capsule, or topical cream for its medicinal uses.
